explicit_text: I was born in Bangkok, Thailand, and spent the first six years of my life there. Even though I was born in the city, I mostly remember traveling back and forth between a refugee camp and another city with my mom. Bangkok represents the beginning of my journey before we moved to the United States.
implicit_text: I was born in the capital city of Thailand and spent the first six years of my life there. Even though I was born in that city, I mostly remember traveling back and forth between a refugee camp and another city with my mom. explicit_text: Thailand was my home for the first six years of my life, where I lived as a refugee with my mom. My parents are Karen, an ethnic group from Burma, and we fled to Thailand because of the ongoing civil war in Burma. Thailand was where my mom worked hard to support me and where we lived in refugee camps before moving to the United States.

explicit_text: My parents are Karen, an ethnic group from Burma, also known as Myanmar. They fled Burma because of the world's longest ongoing civil war, which made life unsafe and difficult. Burma is the place my family originally comes from, but we had to leave to survive.
implicit_text: My parents are Karen, an ethnic group from the country also known as Myanmar. They fled that nation because of the world's longest ongoing civil war, which made life unsafe and difficult. That country is the place my family originally comes from, but we had to leave to survive.

explicit_text: Myanmar is another name for Burma, where my parents are from. The civil war there forced my family to become refugees and seek safety in Thailand. Though I was born in Thailand, my roots and heritage trace back to Myanmar.

explicit_text: The World Relief Organization played a crucial role in our lives by helping us find a sponsor family in the United States. Thanks to them, my mom and I were able to get documented and move to a new country for a better life. Their support was the bridge that allowed us to leave the refugee camp and start fresh in America.

Thanks to their efforts, my mom and I were able to get documented and move to a new country for a better life. Their support was the bridge that allowed us to leave the refugee camp and start fresh in America.

explicit_text: The United States became my new home when I was six years old, thanks to a sponsor family found by World Relief. My mom gave me the gift of a new life here, even though it meant leaving her family and everything familiar behind in Thailand. Growing up in the U.S., I have come to appreciate the sacrifices my mom made for me and the opportunities I now have.
